contents.value: **if** — Liquid keyword.
**elsif** — Liquid keyword.
**else** — Liquid keyword.
**endif** — Liquid keyword.
**unless** — Liquid keyword.
**abs** — Built-in function/command in Liquid.
**append** — Built-in function/command in Liquid.
**at_least** — Built-in function/command in Liquid.
**at_most** — Built-in function/command in Liquid.
**capitalize** — Built-in function/command in Liquid.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

**Flexbox** is a CSS layout model for distributing space along a single axis.
```css
display: flex; justify-content: center;
```

**CSS Grid** is a two-dimensional layout system.
```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr;
```

A **pseudo-class** selects elements in a specific state.
```css
a:hover, input:focus, li:nth-child(2)
```

CSS **custom properties** (variables) store reusable values.
```css
:root { --color: blue; } .el { color: var(--color); }
```
**Specificity** determines which CSS rule applies when multiple rules target the same element. Inline > ID > Class > Tag.

```liquid
{% assign variable = value %}
```
Creates a new named variable and assigns it a value within a Liquid template, persisting for the remainder of the template rendering context.

```liquid
{% capture variable %}...{% endcapture %}
```
Captures the rendered content between the opening and closing tags into a string variable, allowing complex string building from template fragments.

```liquid
{% for item in collection %}...{% endfor %}
```
Iterates over an array or range, rendering the enclosed block for each element. Supports limit, offset, reversed parameters and exposes forloop object with index, first, last properties.

```liquid
{% case variable %}{% when value %}...{% endcase %}
```
Creates a switch-style conditional that checks a variable against multiple when values and renders the matching block, with optional else for default output.

```liquid
{% include 'snippet' %}
```
Inserts the rendered content of another template file into the current template. Supports passing variables with the with keyword for parameterized partial rendering. Deprecated in favor of render.

```liquid
{% render 'snippet', var: value %}
```
Renders a partial template in an isolated scope, preventing it from accessing or modifying the parent template variables. Preferred over include for better encapsulation and performance.

```liquid
{% comment %}...{% endcomment %}
```
Prevents any content between the opening and closing tags from being rendered in the final output, useful for adding developer notes or temporarily disabling template sections.

```liquid
{% raw %}...{% endraw %}
```
Outputs the enclosed content without processing any Liquid tags or objects, allowing display of Liquid code examples or curly-brace syntax in template output.

```liquid
{{ value | prepend: 'string' }} -> String
```
String filter that adds the specified string to the beginning of the input value, useful for prefixing paths, protocols, or labels to existing strings.

```liquid
{{ value | downcase }} -> String
```
Converts all characters in the input string to lowercase, useful for normalizing user input, generating URL slugs, or case-insensitive display formatting.

```liquid
{{ value | upcase }} -> String
```
Converts all characters in the input string to uppercase, commonly used for headings, labels, or emphasis in template output rendering.

```liquid
{{ value | split: 'delimiter' }} -> Array
```
Divides a string into an array of substrings based on the specified delimiter, enabling iteration over comma-separated lists, path segments, or other structured text.

```liquid
{{ array | join: 'glue' }} -> String
```
Combines all elements of an array into a single string, inserting the specified glue string between each element for formatted list output.

```liquid
{{ value | strip_html }} -> String
```
Removes all HTML tags from the input string, producing plain text output suitable for meta descriptions, excerpts, or non-HTML contexts.

```liquid
{{ value | date: 'format' }} -> String
```
Converts a timestamp or date string into a formatted date string using strftime-compatible format directives such as %Y for year, %m for month, and %d for day.

```liquid
{{ array | where: 'property', 'value' }} -> Array
```
Filters an array of objects, returning only those whose specified property matches the given value. Essential for querying collections by attributes in Shopify and Jekyll templates.

```liquid
{{ array | map: 'property' }} -> Array
```
Extracts the values of a specific property from each object in an array, producing a new array of those values for iteration or display.

```liquid
{{ array | sort: 'property' }} -> Array
```
Returns a sorted copy of the input array. When a property name is specified, sorts objects by that property value in ascending order.

```liquid
{{ value | size }} -> Integer
```
Returns the number of characters in a string or the number of elements in an array, commonly used in conditional checks for empty collections or length limits.

```liquid
{{ value | default: 'fallback' }} -> Any
```
Returns the input value if it is present and not empty; otherwise returns the specified fallback value, providing safe defaults for potentially nil or blank variables.

```liquid
{{ value | replace: 'search', 'replacement' }} -> String
```
Replaces all occurrences of the search string in the input with the replacement string, useful for text transformations, sanitization, and URL slug generation.
